prompt: Write the opening paragraph of a children's book about a shy octopus who wants to make friends.
response: In the deepest, quietest corner of the indigo sea, there lived an octopus named Ollie. Ollie had eight splendid, swirly arms, could change his color to match any coral or stone, and was very, very good at hiding. In fact, he was so good at hiding that sometimes, even when he very much wanted to say hello, he would puff into a pebble-gray lump and watch the other sea creatures play from a crack in the reef, wondering what it would be like to have a friend.
